_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the create-cli skill: does a real /charness:create-cli run APPLY its canonical lifecycle-verb convention — emit the canonical `version` surface — rather than just OPEN the doc that describes it? reference-compaction Slice 7 moves this floor from the re-read PROXY to the EMITTED-token floor. Before: requiredCommandFragments pinned four docs including command-conventions.md, whose flagship content — the lifecycle verb enum (init/doctor/update/reset/uninstall/version) and 'treat version as the canonical version surface' — is ALREADY inlined verbatim in skills/public/create-cli/SKILL.md step 2 (lines 44-46), so opening command-conventions.md to satisfy the floor re-reads a convention the run already has in always-loaded core. Fix: requiredSummaryFragments asserts an actually-emitted canonical verb (`version`), re-baselined from a FRESH ask-before-run capture (charness-artifacts/cautilus/create-cli-claim-fidelity-slice7-2026-07-01/), NOT assumed. `version` is NOT in the prompt (which names list/plan/capture/score/doctor), so a run emitting it demonstrably ADDED the canonical version surface from the convention — the exact claim the doc-open proxied. command-conventions.md stays DECLARED with classTag DEPTH: the observed run DID open it, but for its unique depth (probe-surface list, -v=verbose rule, version/help flag conventions, product-shaped-divergence rationale), NOT the inlined verb enum — a depth read opened when the slice needs it, not an every-run floor. create-cli has NO outcome-assertions.json (no substance judge), so `version` carries the emitted signal alone; the three RETAINED RCF floors (intent-first-grammar.md step-2 journey verbs, command-surface.md prep/execute split, quality-gates.md gate axes — all unconditional under the pinned multi-command agent-workflow prompt) hold the routing floor. Honest-reading guard: `version` also occurs incidentally (e.g. a JSON `schema_version`), so it is a FORM floor; a future run that emits `version` only incidentally without a real design still fails the three doc-open floors, and if the token proves too weak in a later capture, re-pin to a stronger emitted phrase — never soften. The prompt names a CONCRETE multi-command agent-driven CLI (an explicit plan=prep / capture=execute split + a doctor lifecycle verb) because two RCF refs are conditional on CLI shape: command-surface.md (prep/execute) and formerly command-conventions.md (multi-command lifecycle) are unconditional only under a multi-command agent-workflow prompt, create-cli's most representative use. declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage; DEPTH-only denominator, INLINE/DUP advisory). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis plus the classTag DUP/INLINE/DEPTH taxonomy. thresholds.max_duration_ms set from this first PASSING baseline capture (177065ms) at ~2x headroom (360000), the hitl/retro model; runtime_budget_respect is a degrade signal, not pass/fail.
prompt: /charness:create-cli  claim-fidelity 픽스처 라이브 캡처를 운영하는 CLI를 설계해줘: list(픽스처 나열) / plan <skill[/scenario]>(캡처 계획 산출=prep) / capture <skill[/scenario]>(슬래시커맨드 실행=execute) / score(observation 평가) / doctor, 에이전트용 --json 출력 포함

case-studies.md.rationale: Not routed by SKILL.md workflow; useful for design context but not required for the ordinary representative run to decide cleanly.
case-studies.md.trigger: when comparing design choices with nearby repos (charness, agent-browser, cautilus) or needing real examples

code-shape.md.rationale: Step 5 says 'keep implementation boring' but does not explicitly route; consulted for hygiene review of parser/handler shape.
code-shape.md.trigger: when implementation structure review is needed or the code shows smell (copied handlers, giant functions, hidden state)

command-conventions.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 routes here for the canonical lifecycle verbs, but that verb enum (init/doctor/update/reset/uninstall/version, 'version as the canonical version surface') is inlined verbatim in SKILL.md step 2 (lines 44-46), so the floor moved off the doc-open to the emitted `version` token (requiredSummaryFragments). This doc stays engage-always DEPTH for its unique depth — probe-surface list, -v=verbose rule, version/help flag conventions, product-shaped-divergence rationale — a read opened for slice depth, not an RCF floor.

command-surface.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 explicitly routes here for prep/execute split decision and multi-command surface design, core to the representative run.

external-capability-clis.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 routes conditionally: 'if the CLI lets agents interact with an external system, define the external capability boundary separately; see references/external-capability-clis.md'.
external-capability-clis.md.trigger: when the CLI reads from or writes to a third-party system like Notion, Linear, GitHub, or Slack

install-update.md.rationale: Step 4 covers distribution contract but does not explicitly route; consulted only when install/update surfaces are in scope.
install-update.md.trigger: when the CLI owns install, update, reset, or lifecycle state mutations

intent-first-grammar.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 2 explicitly routes to this doc to pick journey verbs first and ensure user-facing grammar before locking command names.

machine-readable-state.md.rationale: Step 2 mentions --json and step 3 requires structured outputs from mutations, but the doc is consulted only when agent-readable state matters.
machine-readable-state.md.trigger: when agents consume structured output or the CLI mutates install/support state that later agents must read

quality-gates.md.rationale: SKILL.md step 6 explicitly routes to this doc to choose proof axes and cover gate families before the slice closes.

version-provenance.md.rationale: SKILL.md line 104 mentions recording version provenance and defining cached latest-version checks, but only when the product owns version state.
version-provenance.md.trigger: when update guidance depends on install channel or the CLI needs version-tracking contract
